title: trifolium arvense subsp. longisetum
intro: Trifolium arvense subsp. longisetum, also known as the red clover, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly its high isoflavone content, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of red clover include alleviating symptoms of menopause, osteoporosis, and cardiovascular disease, as well as supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation. Red clover can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as tea, capsules, or tinctures, often used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
